commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: The Lord spoke, (Chaldean; Theodoret) or the angel, (St. Jerome) in human shape. (Haydock) He addresses the prophet, ver. , though what follows immediately seems to belong to the Lord, (Calmet) in whose name he speaks. God hath abandoned the synagogue, but will remain with his Church unto the end, Matthew xxiii. , and xxviii. Yet the perfect Church, without spot, is the ; (Ephesians v. ; Worthington) though the Catholic Church, on earth, is ever holy and "the communion of saints. "(Haydock) Carcasses. Idols, according to some; or rather by the kings being buried on Sion. This is nowhere else reprehended; neither is their building too near the temple, which the prophet here condemns, ver. (Calmet) It seems, however, that if these things had been blameable, such a number of pious and wise kings would not have acted thus, nor the prophets have neglected to admonish them of their duty. The carcasses and houses here specified may have been vestiges of idolatry; or, in future, the tombs and palaces were to be at a more respectful distance.
